titlestmt.titleproper: Gail and Stephen Rudin atomic bomb collection6990
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-09 18:35:51 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Gail and Stephen Rudin atomic bomb collection
did.physdesc: Correspondence, printed materials, models, publications, photographs
did.abstract: Letters, documents, photographs, drawings, and models (signed by Paul Tibbets) related to the atomic bomb and its use in Hiroshima and Nagasaki, Japan.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters, documents, photographs, drawings, and models (signed by Paul Tibbets) related to the atomic bomb and its use in Hiroshima and Nagasaki, Japan.

list.head: Signed Books
list.item: Flight of the Enola Gay, by Paul Tibbets. 1989. Signed by Tibbets, Van Kirk, and Ferebee. Rudin # M-32
Return of the Enola Gay, by Paul Tibbets. 1998. Signed by Tibbets and Van Kirk. Rudin # M-32A
The Tibbets Story, by Paul Tibbets with Clair Stebbins and Harry Franken. Signed by Tibbets, Ferebee and Van Kirk. Rudin # M-32B
509th Pictorial History, by 509th Composite Group. Facsimile edition of 1945 copy. Signed by Tibbets. Rudin # M-32C
Enola Gay, by Gordon Thomas and Max Witts. 1977. Signed and inscribed by Tibbets. Rudin # M-32D

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Gail and Stephen Rudin atomic bomb collection, #6990. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First hand account of the atomic bomb attack on Nagasaki. Rudin # M-1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed Questionnaire regarding Hiroshima. Rudin # M-1B

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Hiroshima: I Saw it First". Bound booklet signed by tail gunner of the Enola Gay. Rudin # M-2

did.unittitle: Caron, George Writer's location: Denver, CO

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Questionnaire/Interview regarding Hiroshima.Rudin # M-2A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Message of the Japanese Home Service after Hiroshima, signed by Ferebee. Rudin # M-3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explanation in his role on the Enola Gay mission. Rudin # M-3A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jeppson's personal thoughts about the Hiroshima mission. Rudin # M-4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "No second wishes about Hiroshima mission - either about selection or results." Rudin # M-4A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: If invasion failed, "Would more atomic bombs have been used in attempts to finally defeat Japan?" Rudin # M-5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reflecting on the Hiroshima mission. Rudin # M-5A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arming and releasing the Hiroshima bomb. Rudin # M-6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jeppson writes a detailed response regarding the Hiroshima mission after being questioned by an admirer. Rudin # M-6B

did.unittitle: Jeppson, Morris Addressee: Dear Sir Writer's location: Hiroshima

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jeppson answers questions of a correspondent about the effects of the bomb on the crew and the destruction and radioactive fallout that were produced. Rudin # M-6C

did.unittitle: Jeppson, Morris Addressee: Dear Sir Writer's location: Hiroshima

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jeppson reveals his thoughts of his mission and describes the use of the atomic bomb. Rudin # M-6D

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reflects on current thinking regarding the use of the atomic bomb. Rudin # M-7A

did.unittitle: Rehl, Donald C. Addressee: Fred D. Cavinder Writer's location: Fountaintown, Indiana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pilot of unused third atomic bomb mission justifies its use. Rudin # M-7

did.unittitle: Sweeney, Charles Writer's location: Nagasaki, Japan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pilot describes shock waves and fireball resulting from the atomic bomb dropped on Nagasaki. Rudin # M-8

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pilot clarifies a historical mistake about his Nagasaki mission - Which plane actually dropped the Nagasaki bomb. Rudin # M-9

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The plane was named "Enola Gay" after my mother," plus a second signature card. Rudin # M-10

did.unittitle: Tibbets, Paul Writer's location: Columbus, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tibbets defends his President's decision to use the atomic bomb. Rudin # M-11

did.unittitle: Tibbets, Paul Writer's location: New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Souvenir facsimile of the front page of the New York Times announcing the bombing of Hiroshima, signed by Paul Tibbets. Rudin # M-12

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Limited edition souvenir typescript (21/24) recalling dropping of the bomb on Hiroshima. Rudin # M-14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reflections on Hiroshima plus signed color photo of Enola Gay. Rudin # M-13

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two identical first day covers, signed by tibbets, with a cachet and stamp. In addition, a full sheet of 36 commemorative stamps. Rudin # M-15B

did.unittitle: Newsmap issued during WWII, a summary of events during the week the Atomic Bomb was dropped on Hiroshima

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rudin # M-15C

did.unittitle: Tibbets, Paul Addressee: General Carl Spaatz Writer's location: Washington, D.C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reproduction of DS giving the directive to proceed with bombing of Japan, weather permitting. Two Copies. Rudin # M-17 and 18

did.unittitle: Tibbets, Paul Addressee: Mayor Timothy Curtin Writer's location: UN, NY and Columbus, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First Day Cover signed by Tibbets and Mitsuo Fuchida, the Japanese commander who led the first wave of attack on Pearl Harbor. Letter regarding Tibbets' second thoughts on dropping the bomb. Rudin # M-19

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with USA Today about the dropping of the atom bomb. Rudin # M-20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter concerning the bombing of Hiroshima.Rudin # M-22

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript covering every aspect of the mission over Hiroshima. Rudin # M-23

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Response to two questions - If Van Kirk agreed to the use of the atomic bomb; and his position on civilian casualties. Rudin # M-24

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed statement entitled "Atomic Bomb Explosion Over Hiroshima". Rudin # M-25

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter concerning the bombing of Hiroshima. Rudin # M-26

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description of the training and preparation for dropping the first atomic bomb over Hiroshima. Rudin # M-27

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter with important details of the dropping of the bomb plus hand-drawn illustration. Rudin # M-28

did.unittitle: Ferebee, Brown Addressee: Writer's location: Tulsa, OK

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mary Babrick Brown donated her hair to be used in a bomb aiming device known as the "Norden Bomb Sight". Rudin # M-28A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopy of the Enola Gay's escape maneuver after dropping the bomb. Rudin # M-29

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed blast damage map of Hiroshima. Rudin # M-30

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: White House Press Release on Hiroshima, "The World Set Free" HG Wells excerpt, the effects of atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki. Signed. Rudin # M-31

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The City of Hiroshima Peace Declaration read by Mayor Setsuo Yamada. Signed by four members of the Enola Gay crew. Rudin # M-31A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Envelope carried in Army Air Force B-29 "Dave's Dream" for Atomic Bomb Test #1. With nine signatures. Rudin # M-40A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Log of observers "Panamint Parade", three newspaper articles. Rudin # M-40

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reproduction of General Order Number 71 awarding the Distinguished Flying Cross to the crew of Bockscar for their mission over Nagasaki. Rudin # M-38

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo of destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped, with inscription by Morris Jeppson. Rudin # M-5B

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed photograph of Tibbets after landing the Enola Gay and accomplishing Hiroshima bomb mission. Shown receiving Distinguished Service Cross from General Carl Spaatz. Rudin # M-15

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three items: First day of non-issue cover, signed photo of Tibbets being decorated after Hiroshima, Photo of Japanese surrender signed by Tibbets Rudin # M-15A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed 8.5"x11" photo of Enola Gay by Paul Tibbets. (Over bomb pit) Also included is a letter explaining the "R" tail marking. Rudin # M-16

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed and inscribed photo of Enola Gay. Rudin # M-29A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed photo of Enola Gay coming in for a landing. Rudin # M-29B

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed photo of mushroom cloud over Hiroshima signed by Tibbets, Van Kirk and Ferebee. Two different photos. Rudin # M-33 and 34

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enola Gay signed photo by Tibbets, Van Kirk and Ferebee. Rudin # M-35

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed photo of Hiroshima mushroom cloud signed by Tibbets, Van Kirk and Ferebee. Plus signed photo of the crew members - Tibbets, Van Kirk and Ferebee. Rudin # M-35A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed photo with descriptions of the two atom bombs that were dropped on Japan. Signed by Tibbets, Jeppson, Sweeney, and Ashworth. Rudin # M-36

did.unittitle: Tibbets, Van Kirk, Jeppson, Ferebee, Nelson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aerial view of Hiroshima in the winter of 1945-46. Signed in blue felt tip by crew members Rudin # M-36B

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aerial photo of Hiroshima after the destruction of the A-bomb with inscription by Van Kirk.Rudin # M-36C

did.unittitle: Tibbets, Van Kirk, Ferebee, Jeppson, Nelson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed Enola Gay photo, Tibbets being decorated after mission, and bomber signed by Tibbets.Rudin # M-37

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photo of Bockscar on tarmac signed by Olivi and Sweeney. Rudin # M-38A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lot of 15 photos after the dropping of the atomic bomb on Hiroshima. Images contain devastated scenery and buildings, as well as photos of survivors and military personnel. (See captions on verso on some of the photos). Rudin # M-39

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three original wire photos: two Nagasaki bomb blast photos, Hiroshima devastation. Rudin # M-39A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 photographs from Operation Crossroads, the United States' 1946 nuclear bomb tests. Rudin # M-40

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten explanations of the components and how it works. Signed by Morris Jeppson. Rudin # M-6A

did.unittitle: Wooden model of the B-29 Enola Gay

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/72 scale wooden model of the Enola Gay, signed on the wing by Tibbets. On wooden display stand and accompanied by a photo of Tibbets signing. Rudin # M-10A

did.unittitle: Wooden model of the B-29 Enola Gay

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/72 scale wooden model of the Enola Gay, signed on the wing by Tibbets. On wooden display stand and accompanied by a photo of Tibbets signing. Rudin # M-10A

did.unittitle: Plastic model of the "Little Boy" bomb

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cast plastic scale model of the "Little Boy" atomic bomb dropped over Hiroshima signed by Paul Tibbets. Rudin # M-10B

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten explanations of the components and how it works by Van Kirk.Rudin # M-21

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Log of the Enola Gay entirely filled out in the hand of Van Kirk. Rudin # M-21A
